article_title: Replimune's Oncolytic Virus Achieves 47.8% Three-Year Survival in Checkpoint-Resistant Melanoma as Company Lines Up Third FDA Submission
summary: Rigorous clinical data reporting with all survival statistics, patient demographics, and quotes verified across 5 sources; automated warning is a domain allowlist gap only.

content_preview.title: Replimune's Oncolytic Virus Achieves 47.8% Three-Year Survival in Checkpoint-Resistant Melanoma as Company Lines Up Third FDA Submission
content_preview.summary: IGNYTE trial data show 83.5% of responders alive at three years; Replimune plans third BLA resubmission after two FDA rejections.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

An engineered herpes simplex virus combined with a checkpoint inhibitor kept nearly half of patients with checkpoint-resistant advanced melanoma alive at three years — a result that Replimune's chief medical officer called "rarely seen" in this historically treatment-resistant setting. The three-year landmark survival analysis from the IGNYTE phase 3 trial (NCT03767348) was presented in an oral session at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting on May 30...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent clinical reporting on a significant oncology data readout. The article accurately reports the IGNYTE three-year survival analysis with appropriate use of confidence intervals and subgroup data. The regulatory history is clearly documented. Good use of 'What We Don't Know' to flag the unresolved FDA situation.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (Replimune GlobeNewswire press release, ASCO presentation): confirms 47.8% three-year OS across all patients, 83.5% of responders alive at 3 years, median OS 32.9 months, ORR 33.6%, median DOR 24.8 months, 44.8% responders at 3 years, Kostas Xynos CMO quote verbatim. source-1.html.gz (CancerNetwork detailed analysis): confirms all annual OS rates (75.3%/61.5%/47.8%), 95% CI values (25.8–46.0), non-responder median OS 18.5 months, patient demographics (n=140, median age 62, 67.9% male, 49.3% stage IVM1b/c/d, 63.6% BRAF wild-type), stratification by tumor location (104 superficial-only, 14 deep+superficial, 22 deep/visceral only), safety data (88.7% any grade, 12.8% grade 3/4, chills/fatigue/pyrexia as top toxicities, no grade 5 events), Michael K. Wong MD quote verbatim. source-2.html.gz (Replimune GlobeNewswire BLA resubmission announcement): confirms May 29 announcement, planned resubmission 'in the coming days', FDA prioritized review language, CEO Sushil Patel quote verbatim. source-3.html.gz (CancerNetwork, October 2025): confirms PDUFA date April 10, 2026 for second resubmission (Class 2 timeline), July 2025 first CRL citing inadequate clinical investigation and heterogeneity, October 2025 resubmission. source-4.html.gz (RTTNews): corroborates key statistics, no claims added from this source specifically.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All clinical data points verified with exact values. Article describes the article mentions 'phase 1/2 IGNYTE trial' in the regulatory history section — source-3 and source-1 both confirm the first CRL cited the 'phase 1/2 IGNYTE trial' was deemed inadequate (the trial transitioned from phase 1/2 to phase 3). The article correctly uses CancerNetwork as the source for the phase characterization. All survival statistics carry proper confidence intervals from source-1.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Company claims are appropriately attributed. Regulatory setbacks are accurately reported without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Replimune/IGNYTE coverage found in archive. Original topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exceptionally well-sourced clinical reporting. All data verified against primary sources (company press releases, CancerNetwork peer-reviewed conference coverage). The single automated warning (rttnews.com not on allowlist) is administrative. Upgrading to APPROVE.
